variants.note: can also mean angry
also means awkward
prefix: re lindo, re bueno
used between friends
friendly between mates
context-dependent
context-dependent

concepts.meaning_en: to screw up, to blow it
to nail it, to get it right

variants.note: very Colombian
very strong
very strong
very strong
lazy in this context

concepts.meaning_en: to goof off, waste time, mess around

variants.note: strong
extremely versatile, can mean anything
the ultimate filler word

concepts.meaning_en: to mess with, joke, pull someone's leg
I don't care, whatever, couldn't care less

variants.note: strong

concepts.meaning_en: to hang out, chill with friends
your squad, crew, group of friends
to gossip, talk trash about someone
to leave quickly, bounce, take off

variants.note: quedó picado = got salty about it
lit. burning; can't let go of an offense

concepts.meaning_en: gut feeling, hunch — an intuitive sense or vibe about something

variants.note: me tinca = I have a feeling / I dig it; ¿te tinca? = are you down?
pololear = to date someone
from San Mateo, the diligent apostle

concepts.meaning_en: bullshitter, someone who wildly exaggerates or makes things up

variants.note: from chamuyo = smooth talk
ese gallo es bien choro = that guy is really gutsy

concepts.meaning_en: intense, wild, over the top, extreme

variants.note: esa situación estuvo cuática = that situation was intense/wild

concepts.meaning_en: fraud, fake person — someone who talks big but never delivers

variants.note: lit. fallen from the attic; oblivious and out of touch

concepts.meaning_en: to flirt with or hit on someone romantically

variants.note: lit. to throw the dogs at someone
to send vibes, make a move

concepts.meaning_en: resilient, tough, impressive — someone who pushes through anything

variants.note: also as exclamation: ¡qué berraquera! = how impressive!

concepts.meaning_en: worthless, pathetic, of very poor quality or character

variants.note: also for a person who is a pushover or disappointment

concepts.meaning_en: settled in, comfortable, feeling at home in a new place or situation

variants.note: ya me amañé aquí = I've settled in nicely here

concepts.meaning_en: to leave yourself open, be careless and vulnerable to being taken advantage of

variants.note: used as warning: no dé papaya — don't make yourself an easy target

concepts.meaning_en: mom or dad, parent — informal address

variants.note: los cuchos = parents; cucho = old man/dad, cucha = mom/old woman

concepts.meaning_en: hooked, obsessed — deeply invested in something or head over heels for someone

variants.note: estoy encarretado con esa serie = I'm hooked on that show
often romantic but also works for things

concepts.meaning_en: fake, counterfeit, inauthentic — object or person

variants.note: used for counterfeit goods or people who are inauthentic

concepts.meaning_en: smooth talk, charming BS used to seduce or con someone

variants.note: chamuyar = the verb; classic porteño pickup/con-artist energy

concepts.meaning_en: a favor, a solid done out of solidarity

variants.note: from gaucho culture — helping with no strings attached
hacerle un paro = to do someone a solid

concepts.meaning_en: bad energy, negative atmosphere from a person or place
the police, the cops — informal or pejorative

variants.note: vesre (syllable reversal) of policía
from the tan uniform color; chilango slang

concepts.meaning_en: done with such ease it looks effortless — pulled off smoothly

variants.note: from the backheel goal in soccer — executed so smoothly it seems accidental

concepts.meaning_en: nightclub, dance bar, place to party at night

variants.note: in Argentina exclusively means nightclub; elsewhere it means small shop
standard Spanish antro = dive bar, but MX slang for any nightclub
lit. shat yourself; used for extreme fear
lit. with hair standing on end

concepts.meaning_en: kid, teenager, young person — casual address

variants.note: norteño slang: Sinaloa, Sonora, Jalisco

concepts.meaning_en: bribe, under-the-table payment to an official

variants.note: lit. the bite; iconic for petty corruption, especially traffic cops

concepts.meaning_en: in trouble, screwed, in a bad spot

variants.note: lit. in the oven
done for; softer substitute for chingado

concepts.meaning_en: to tease, roast, or rib someone — usually affectionately among friends

variants.note: le echaron carrilla toda la noche = they roasted him all night
me voy a mi jato = I'm going home; quintessential limeño slang

concepts.meaning_en: to chip in, pool money together among a group

variants.note: hacer una chancha = to pass the hat / chip in together
hacer una vaquita = to chip in

concepts.meaning_en: to notice, spot, or catch on to something

variants.note: mánya eso = check that out; from Italian immigrant mangiare, repurposed in Lima street speech

concepts.meaning_en: to fail a test or class

variants.note: jalado/a = failed; me jalaron = they failed me; unique Peruvian academic usage

concepts.meaning_en: to flatter insincerely, sweet-talk someone with hollow compliments

variants.note: floreo = hollow flattery; no me floreés = don't sweet-talk me

concepts.meaning_en: first-rate, excellent, the absolute real deal

variants.note: superlative praise; used when something or someone is genuinely top-tier

concepts.meaning_en: fair-skinned or light-haired person — used as casual address

variants.note: common term of address, not necessarily derogatory

concepts.meaning_en: jealous, controlling, or possessive person — especially a romantic partner

variants.note: also the name of a venomous Venezuelan viper; me tiene una cuaima encima = my partner is suffocating me with jealousy

concepts.meaning_en: weak, spineless, lacking substance or conviction

variants.note: from the iconic thin Venezuelan drip coffee; ese man es un guayoyo = that guy has no backbone

concepts.meaning_en: to shake someone down for a bribe, especially corrupt police demanding payment

variants.note: also dar la matraca; los tombos me matraquearon = the cops shook me down

concepts.meaning_en: foreigner, outsider — someone not from here

variants.note: from French monsieur via European immigrants; ese musiú no entiende nada = that foreigner doesn't get it
